channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Dozens of religious groups sue to stop Trump admin from arresting migrants in places of worship
item.description: A coalition of 27 Christian and Jewish groups filed a lawsuit challenging a Trump administration action allowing immigration agents to make arrests in places of worship.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ex-NY Giants player is helping deported migrants in Guatemala, blames Biden for the problem
item.description: Former New York Giants safety Jack Brewer tells Fox News Digital he is in Guatemala helping the return of deported migrants to the country and uncovering lies they've been told.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New resistance battling Trump's second term through onslaught of lawsuits taking aim at EOs
item.description: Fox New Digital compiled a list of groups, elected officials, individuals and local jurisdictions that have launched lawsuits against the Trump administration over executive actions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia releases imprisoned American Marc Fogel in what US calls a step toward the end of Ukraine war
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Marc Fogel, an American history teacher who was deemed wrongfully detained by Russia, has been released and returned to the U.S. in what the White House described as a diplomatic thaw that could advance negotiations to end the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration battles employee lawsuit to block dismantling of USAID
item.description: The Trump administration will present an unforgiving argument for dismantling the U.S. Agency for International Development to a federal judge.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Knickmeyer And Lindsay Whitehurst, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Appeals court rejects Trump administration push to reinstate spending freezes on grants and loans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal appeals court on Tuesday rejected a Trump administration push to reinstate a sweeping pause on federal funding, a decision that comes after a judge found the administration had not fully obeyed an earlier order. The Boston-based 1st U.S.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gov. Hochul reacts to pending dismissal of corruption case against Mayor Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- The pending dismissal of the corruption case against New York City Mayor Eric Adams has partners in running the city and political opponents raising concerns about whether Adams can run the city. Gov. Kathy Hochul even said she does not know if the mayor is “compromised.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Democrat senator backs Trump's 'common sense move' to fire the penny
item.description: Sen. Jacky Rosen came out in support of President Donald Trump's plan to end production of the penny, which she agreed would save money.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump signs order instructing DOGE to massively cut federal workforce
item.description: President Donald Trump expected to sign an executive order Tuesday instructing the Department of Government Efficiency to plan for cuts to federal workforce staffing numbers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hegseth didn't request over $137,000 in upgrades to his new military residence, official says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth did not request more than $137,000 in repairs and upgrades to military housing at Fort McNair that will become his official residence, according to a U.S.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ex-FEMA worker who directed staff to avoid homes with Trump signs violated Hatch Act, agency says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal agency is seeking disciplinary action against a former Federal Emergency Management Agency worker who directed workers helping hurricane survivors to avoid homes with yard signs supporting President Donald Trump, according to a complaint filed Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Schumer: Democrats will use spending bills to curb Musk
item.description: Senate Democratic Leader Chuck Schumer (N.Y.) says Democrats will push for language in the spending bills that need to pass by March 14 that would unwind Elon Musk’s efforts to dismantle government agencies such as the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge adjusts ruling blocking Musk, DOGE from Treasury Department payment systems
item.description: A federal judge Tuesday clarified that a ruling preventing Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) from accessing the Treasury Department’s payment systems does not extend to Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: 27 religious groups sue Trump administration over immigration enforcement policy
item.description: 27 religious groups are suing the federal government in response to the Trump administration’s policy giving immigration agents more leeway to make arrests at “sensitive locations” like churches.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Ventre, MPR News

item.title: The pope rebukes Trump over migrant deportations and refutes VP Vance’s theology
item.description: In a strongly worded letter to Catholic bishops in the United States, Pope Francis is taking to task the Trump administration’s stance on migrants, calling it a “major crisis.
item.dc:creator: Jason DeRose, MPR News

item.title: Durbin calls for investigation into Patel's role in FBI firings
item.description: Sen. Dick Durbin (D-Ill.) is asking the Department of Justice’s (DOJ) inspector general to investigate “highly credible information” that Kash Patel, President Trump’s pick to head the FBI, is already directing a “purge” of the bureau before he’s been confirmed by the Senate.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: It’s the last week to change party affiliation ahead of NYC mayor’s race
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – Friday is the last day to change your party registration ahead of the 2025 mayoral election. In June, New York City voters will pick their party’s candidate for the mayoral election in November.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Hegseth says DOGE welcome at Pentagon as Defense Department reviews military posture globally
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Pentagon did not push same "globalist agendas" as USAID but welcomed DOGE "partnership" to find billions in waste.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jordan pledges to take in sick Palestinian children as Trump backpedals on aid threat
item.description: King Abdullah II of Jordan pledged to take in 2,000 sick Palestinian children from the Gaza Strip on Tuesday amid pressure from President Trump for his country to take in Palestinians from the enclave.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: JD Vance, Treasury Sec Scott Bessent to meet with Zelenskyy as Trump team sets sights on Russia-Ukraine war
item.description: Vice President JD Vance will meet with President Volodymyr Zelenskyy on Friday after years of railing against the U.S.’ continued funding of Ukraine in the war against Russia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: JD Vance rails against 'excessive' AI regulation in a rebuke to Europe at the Paris AI summit
item.description: PARIS (AP) — U.S. Vice President JD Vance on Tuesday warned global leaders and tech industry executives that “excessive regulation” could cripple the rapidly growing artificial intelligence industry in a rebuke to European efforts to curb AI’s risks.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS ADAMSON and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump ally Steve Bannon pleads guilty and avoids jail time in border wall fraud case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Steve Bannon pleaded guilty on Tuesday to defrauding donors to a private effort to build a wall on the U.S. southern border, ending a case the conservative strategist decried as a “political persecution.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump says he will impose a 25% tariff on aluminum and steel imports
item.description: Additionally, he plans to announce reciprocal tariffs on "everyone" on Tuesday or Wednesday: "If they charge us, we charge them. That's all," he said. "Every country will be reciprocal.
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff, MPR News
